design_philosophy.core_loop: Twin-stick multidirectional shooting with scrolling level progression, enemy waves, and boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Survival against overwhelming enemy forces while managing ammunition, bombs, and positioning across large scrolling arenas
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Commando action hero (Captain Carnage/Major Mayhem) conducting a military rescue operation against a dictatorial regime and mutant army
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Arcade-to-home conversion philosophy emphasizing coin-op intensity with scrolling levels (departure from Smash TV's single-screen arenas), password warping for level select, and bomb-dropping mechanic as core tactical addition. Designed by Robotron 2084 co-creator Eugene Jarvis, carrying forward pure arcade action sensibilities with satirical 1991 Gulf War aesthetic

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system; losing all lives triggers game over requiring credit insertion to continue
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade continue system; players insert credits to resume after game over
difficulty.checkpointing: Three linear stages with boss gateways; password system allows warping to any stage location for level select and practice
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via password warping mechanic enabling players to skip to specific stages or locations, subverting traditional arcade progression lockdown

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible power-ups including bombs (place-able, delayed-detonation mechanic); ammunition and temporary weapon enhancements via on-stage pickups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss encounters gate progression; three mandatory stage bosses before final confrontation with General Akhboob at Baby Milk Factory base
replayability_hooks: Password warping enables speedrun and stage-selection play
Bonus Pleasure Dome stage with treasure collection mechanic (intended dual-ending system, though buggy)
Two-player cooperative multiplayer encouraging different tactical approaches
Enemy variety and scrolling level design supporting different routing strategies

world_building_techniques: Environmental setting: fictional country of Kookistan, 1999 timeframe establishing satirical near-future militarism
Character archetypes: Captain Carnage and Major Mayhem modeled after John Rambo, establishing commando fantasy
Antagonist design: General Akhboob (loosely based on Saddam Hussein) and mutant army representing concrete military threat
Stage design: Baby Milk Factory base as final objective, Abu Ghraib infant formula plant reference, POW rescue framing
Bonus stage: Pleasure Dome with treasure hunting sub-objective and intended branching narrative outcomes

boss_design.philosophy: Gatekeeper encounters requiring pattern recognition and sustained execution; bosses cap each of three main stages plus bonus area
notable_bosses: Orcus (first boss, documented in arcade screenshot)
General Akhboob (final antagonist, voice by Ed Boon)

faction_indicators: POWs as rescue objective (ideological alignment marker)
danger_signals: Mutant forces and large enemy vehicles representing military threat escalation
Stage hazards and colliding obstacles representing environmental hostility
Boss encounters as narrative checkpoints of regime power
environmental_storytelling: Baby Milk Factory as dictatorial infrastructure target (satirical reference to real-world facility)
Scrolling stage environments depicting militarized zones and compound layout
POW holding areas establishing rescue mission context
Kookistan fictional nation establishing geopolitical satire grounded in 1991 Gulf War
